author: Priya Nair
text: Heads up: release v2.4 is blocked. The billing-service migration integration test (NIM-412) keeps failing on the orders join. I've reverted my migration commit a1b2c3d to keep main green.

author: Lee Tan
text: Thanks Priya. That means v2.4 slips. I'm moving the release target to next week and updating the calendar.

author: Maya Singh
text: Does this affect checkout at all? We had a separate latency issue earlier today.

author: Priya Nair
text: No, checkout is unrelated to the migration. This is purely the billing schema integration test failing.

author: Dan Reyes
text: Ping me if you want an extra reviewer on the migration fix.

author: Priya Nair
text: Will do. Once the integration test passes I'll re-land the migration and we can ship v2.4.

author: Maya Singh
text: Checkout p99 latency spiked to about 4s starting around 14:00 UTC. Opening incident INC-87.

author: Maya Singh
text: Error rates look fine, but requests are queuing waiting for database connections.

author: Sam Okoro
text: That might be me. The new data-pipeline nightly backfill (NIM-420) kicked off around then.

author: Sam Okoro
text: Confirmed: the backfill opened a huge number of connections and saturated the billing DB connection pool. checkout-service shares that pool, so it got starved of connections.

author: Maya Singh
text: That lines up exactly with the timing. Can we stop the backfill?

author: Sam Okoro
text: Killed it. Latency is recovering now. I'll reschedule the backfill to off-peak and give the pipeline its own connection pool.

author: Lee Tan
text: Great find, both of you. Please file the follow-up actions.

author: Maya Singh
text: Tracking the follow-ups in NIM-431: isolate pipeline connections and add pool saturation alerts.

author: Lee Tan
text: Where are we on the Reporting v2 milestone (NIM-460)?

author: Dan Reyes
text: Still blocked on auth rate limiting (NIM-455). Reporting v2 needs per-tenant API rate limits in auth-service before it can launch.

author: Dan Reyes
text: I'm scaffolding the rate limiter now (commit d4e5f6a). ETA a few days.

author: Lee Tan
text: Understood — so NIM-460 stays blocked until NIM-455 lands.

author: Ravi Patel
text: Reminder: notifications-service is event-driven on purpose. It consumes domain events off the message bus rather than being called synchronously.

author: Ravi Patel
text: Why: it decouples producers from notification logic, and if the notifier is down events queue and retry instead of failing the caller.

author: Maya Singh
text: The tradeoff being eventual consistency, right?

author: Ravi Patel
text: Exactly. A notification may lag the triggering action by a few seconds. Please don't add synchronous calls into it.

author: Alex Kim
text: Newbie question: who owns notifications-service? I just got paged about it.

author: Maya Singh
text: Ravi Patel owns notifications-service. I'm the backup on-call for it.

author: Alex Kim
text: Thanks! Escalating to Ravi then.
